title: Backtracking, Constraint Propagation, and Branch-and-Bound · Adversarial-Input Lab
summary: Search schedules and assignments while proving why pruned branches cannot help. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a bounded combinatorial solver with pruning audit with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define partial assignments, legal extensions, completion, and undo invariants.
Prune only with a condition that cannot remove a valid or better solution.
Use constrained-first and value-order heuristics without confusing speed with correctness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
